Avetta PQF - Germany Facility Operating Permit

The Germany Operating Facility Permit is an authorization required for certain facilities to commence and continue operations in compliance with occupational health, safety, and environmental protection requirements. Its purpose is to ensure that a facility is designed, maintained, and operated in such a manner that risks to employees, the environment, and the general public are minimized or eliminated according to statutory requirements. The permit serves as formal approval that a business location, equipment, or type of installation meets all relevant technical, safety, and legal standards prior to and during operation. Federal Emission Control Act Sections 4 and 16 Industrial Safety Ordinance Occupational Safety and Health Act Federal Emission Control Act
